sidebar.title: winmux
pane.connect.persistent_hint: Wrap remote shell in tmux so reconnects resume the same session
pane.meta.title_placeholder: title (e.g. trying to find the X bug)
pane.meta.annot_placeholder: annotation (longer free text — context, intent, links)
pane.meta.hint: Enter to save title; Ctrl+Enter to save from annotation; Esc to cancel
host_trust.warn: The server's host key is different from the one we trusted before. This may indicate a man-in-the-middle attack — or the server was rekeyed.
settings.title: Settings
settings.terminal.allow_proposed: Allow xterm.js proposed API (needed for WebGL)
settings.hooks.title: Agent hooks
settings.hooks.enabled: Pipe AI agent permission requests through winmux
settings.hooks.cli_hint: To install/refresh the OS-level hook entries, run {cmd} in any terminal.
settings.notifications.toast_enabled: Show OS toast notifications (workspace events, updates)
settings.updates.title: Update check
settings.language.title: Language
settings.language.label: Language
notes.title: Notes
notes.placeholder: Drop an idea, bug, or todo… Ctrl+Enter to save.
browser.waiting_ssh: Waiting for SSH session to come up…
browser.empty_url: Enter a URL above to load a page.
ws.create.field.name: Name
ws.create.auth.label: Authentication
ws.create.auth.password.hint: You'll be prompted for the password each time you connect. The password is never saved.
ws.create.field.setup_cmd.placeholder: run after the shell prompt is ready, e.g. 'cd /repo && nvm use'
wizard.picker.title: Pick a host from ~/.ssh/config
wizard.picker.empty: No Host blocks found. Add some to ~/.ssh/config to use this.
provisioning.title: Provision new server
provisioning.hint.connect: Enter the initial credentials your hosting provider gave you (commonly root + password). winmux will inspect the server before any mutation.
provisioning.steps.title: Steps (uncheck to skip)
provisioning.run.label: Run: {id} · target {host}
provisioning.done.message: Provisioning finished. You can close this dialog.
fm.sort.label: Sort
fm.sort.name: Name
fm.empty.no_ssh: Remote unavailable — connect an SSH terminal pane in this workspace first.
fm.action.prompt_local:
{name} — type:
  o = open (cd into directory / open file with default app)
  e = edit (built-in editor)
  u = upload to remote
  r = rename
  d = delete
fm.action.prompt_remote:
{name} — type:
  o = open (cd into directory / download to temp and open file)
  e = edit (built-in editor)
  d = download to local
  r = rename
  x = delete
settings.font.web.title: Web font (optional)
settings.font.web.hint: Pastes a CSS link tag at runtime. After it loads, type the family name in the fields above (e.g. {example}).
settings.terminal.rtl.title: Hebrew / Arabic (RTL)
settings.terminal.rtl.hint: How to display lines that contain right-to-left text. Switching the mode affects newly-opened panes immediately; existing panes update the write pipeline live but keep their original renderer.
settings.terminal.rtl.auto.label: Per-line auto (Termius-style)
settings.terminal.rtl.auto.desc: DOM renderer; each line gets dir="auto" and the browser picks direction by the first strong character. Best default for SSH prompts that occasionally print Hebrew.
settings.terminal.rtl.bidi.label: Full BiDi reorder (legacy)
settings.terminal.rtl.bidi.desc: WebGL renderer + bidi-js logical→visual reorder. Faster paint, but rewrites the byte stream — editable lines may surprise.
settings.terminal.rtl.off.label: Off
settings.terminal.rtl.off.desc: WebGL renderer, no reorder. Hebrew bytes render left-to-right as written.
settings.terminal.use_winmux_tmux_config: Bundles a tmux.conf at ~/.winmux/tmux.conf so wheel scrolls the scrollback ring instead of shell history, 50k-line buffer, sane truecolour. Turn off to fall back to your own ~/.tmux.conf. Takes effect on next pane connect.
settings.autoConnect.label: Auto-connect on workspace select
settings.autoConnect.hint: When enabled, switching to an SSH workspace establishes a background connection so tmux sessions and your files are immediately visible. Disable to defer the connection until a terminal pane is opened.
ws_header.split_filemanager_title: Split into file manager pane (local + SFTP)
sidebar.provision_server_tooltip: Bootstrap a fresh server with users, keys, and tools
settings.updates.logs.title: Logs
sidebar.ports.label: Ports
sidebar.ports.tooltip: Lets your local browser reach what's running on the remote server
notes.window.title: Notes — {workspace}
workspace.delete.notesWarning: This workspace has {count} note(s) that will be deleted. Continue?
ports.window.title: Ports — {workspace}
ports.window.toggle.label: Auto-forward listening ports
ports.window.empty.activeNoForwards: No active forwards. Run a server on the remote to see them here.
ports.window.empty.toggleOff: Auto port forwarding is off for this workspace. Toggle on to start tracking.
ports.window.empty.noWorkspace: Select a workspace to manage its port forwards.
ws.create.shell.label: Shell
ws.create.cwd.label: Working directory
ws.create.custom_cmd.label: Command
ws.create.custom_cmd.placeholder: e.g. pwsh.exe -NoLogo, wsl bash -l, cmd.exe /K cd
provisioning.agents.help: All optional. Codex and Gemini require Node.js (enable that step too).
provisioning.step.install_claude.label: Claude Code (Anthropic) — curl installer
provisioning.step.install_codex.label: Codex CLI (OpenAI) — npm
provisioning.step.install_gemini.label: Gemini CLI (Google) — npm
provisioning.step.install_claude.auth_hi: Run `claude` on the server to authenticate (browser), or set ANTHROPIC_API_KEY for headless mode.
provisioning.step.install_codex.auth_hin: Run `codex login --device-auth` on the server for headless auth (or `codex login` if you have a desktop browser).
provisioning.step.install_gemini.auth_hi: Run `gemini` to sign in with Google, or set GEMINI_API_KEY for headless mode.
provisioning.step.add_winmux_to_path.des: Append ~/.winmux/bin to the user's shell rc file (bash / zsh / fish). Idempotent.
provisioning.done.workspace_skipped: Provisioning ended without a working key login — no workspace was created. Re-run the wizard or create one manually.
prov.error.sudoRequired.title: Passwordless sudo required
prov.error.sudoRequired.body: Provisioning installs packages and writes to /etc, which needs root. The login user can't run sudo without a password — log in as root, or add this line to /etc/sudoers (use `sudo visudo`):
prov.error.stepFailed.title: Step failed: {step}
sshKey.offer.title: Set up SSH key authentication?
sshKey.offer.body: Generate an ed25519 keypair and install the public key on the server. Next connection will use the key — no password typing, faster, more reliable.
sshKey.offer.warning: Note: this does NOT grant the user sudo privileges. Server provisioning still requires passwordless sudo on the remote.
sshKey.offer.password: Confirm SSH password (used once to install the key):
sshKey.offer.success: Key installed — next connection will use it.
ws.autoPortForward.label: Auto-forward listening ports
ws.autoPortForward.hint: When a server starts listening on the remote, winmux opens a local SSH tunnel automatically. Click ports in the sidebar to open them in your browser.
ports.panel.title: Ports
ports.panel.empty: No ports forwarded — start a server in your shell to see it here
ports.notification.opened: localhost:{local} ready (from remote :{remote}) — click to open
settings.shortcuts.title: Keyboard shortcuts
settings.shortcuts.hint: Click a binding to record a new combination. Esc cancels. Modifier-only chords (Ctrl alone, etc.) are rejected.
settings.shortcuts.ctrl_c_copy: When text is selected, Ctrl+C copies instead of sending SIGINT
fm.empty.connect_terminal_first: Connect a terminal pane in this workspace to load remote files.
fm.toast.opened_remote: Downloaded to temp and opened: {file} (changes won't sync back)
editor.title: {filename} — Editor
editor.large_file_warning: File is large ({size}). Editing may be slow.
settings.claude.title: Claude session summary
settings.claude.hint: Pipe the last N exchanges of a Claude Code conversation through `claude -p` on the remote, save the result as a winmux note tagged `summary`. Manual trigger: keyboard shortcut. Automatic: opt in below.
settings.claude.auto_on_stop: Auto-summarize when Claude Code finishes a session (Stop hook)
settings.claude.prompt_hint: Use {N} as a placeholder for the history count (substituted at run time).
hooks_update.banner.title: Hook spec update available
hooks_update.banner.text: {agent} hooks v{current} → v{latest} on this server
hooks_update.toast_done: Hooks updated to v{version}. Restart your agent to take effect.
settings.hooks_updates.title: Hook update banners
settings.hooks_updates.show_banners: Show a banner when an agent's hook spec is outdated
fm.action.create_file_prompt: New file name (will be created empty):
fm.btn.add_menu: Add… (new folder / new file / upload from disk)
fm.btn.upload_from_disk_local: Add file from disk → local folder
editor.dir.title: Text direction (auto follows document language)
tmux_picker.title: Attach to tmux session
tmux_picker.new_session_hint: Create a fresh tmux session named after this pane.
tmux_picker.empty: No existing sessions — pick "New session" above.
tmux_picker.invalid_name: Name can't contain spaces, dots, or colons.
claude_picker.title: Resume Claude Code session
claude_picker.empty: No Claude Code sessions found on this host.
tmux_picker.new_session_hint_v2: Create a fresh session with a unique auto-generated name
_phase24d_note: Phase 24.D removed ws_header.add_chat / .add_claudelog and the chat.* / cl.* key namespaces with the ClaudeChat (Phase 22) + ClaudeLog (Phase 24.B) panes.
